docs.title: ML Portfolio - DALIS
docs.text:
A clean, technical portfolio website for machine learning, quantitative finance, and research-driven trading strategy development.

A modern showcase of model engineering, signal research, and portfolio-level analysis for recruiters, hiring managers, and quantitative teams.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
This documentation-style portfolio presents four core capabilities:

  • Time series forecasting for volatility and return behavior
  • Factor modeling using alternative data and market signals
  • Backtesting of strategy performance, risk, and trade economics
  • Quantitative research combining sentiment, correlations, and statistical validation

docs.title: About Me
docs.text:
I am a quantitative researcher and machine learning practitioner specializing in data-driven finance. My portfolio emphasizes:

  • Technical rigor in model design and evaluation
  • Financial intuition for market behavior and risk
  • Production-aware architecture for reproducibility and deployment
  • Recruiter-ready storytelling through polished research documentation

docs.title: Featured Projects
docs.text: Project Focus Value Proposition Volatility Forecasting ML + GARCH + Monte Carlo Predicts risk and tail outcomes with ensemble models and volatility dynamics Signal Backtesting Long/flat SPY strategy Evaluates real-world strategy performance, Sharpe, drawdown, and trade statistics Correlation Research Google Trends + stock prices Finds statistically significant relationships between macro/search sentiment and equities Sentiment Scanner Hybrid fundamental + technical + social signals Generates actionable signals from news, Reddit, and technical indicators

docs.title: Tech Stack
docs.text:
• Languages: Python, Markdown
  • Data: pandas, numpy, yfinance, Polygon.io, Google Trends
  • Models: scikit-learn, arch, Random Forest, Gradient Boosting, Logistic Regression
  • Visualization: matplotlib, seaborn
  • Documentation: MkDocs, Material theme, GitHub Pages

docs.title: Deployment
docs.text: This portfolio is optimized for GitHub Pages and MkDocs.

docs.title: Local preview
docs.text:
pip install mkdocs mkdocs-material
mkdocs serve

Open the local preview at http://127.0.0.1:8000.

docs.title: Deploy to GitHub Pages
docs.text:
mkdocs gh-deploy --force

The site publishes to:

https://jdurairaj-hub.github.io/ml-portfolio-dalis/

docs.title: GitHub Actions
docs.text: A workflow is already configured at .github/workflows/gh-pages.yml to build and deploy automatically on pushes to main.

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• The site uses a dark technical theme for professional presentation.
  • Navigation is optimized for recruiters and quantitative decision-makers.
  • Project pages include architecture, metrics, and cross-domain research context.

docs.title: Backtesting
docs.text: Rigorous backtesting is essential for validating portfolio strategy performance and risk management assumptions.

docs.title: Strategy focus
docs.text:
• prediction-model/: long/flat SPY strategy using logistic regression and technical features
  • Performance analysis includes Sharpe ratio, max drawdown, and win rate

docs.title: Backtest process
docs.text:
1. Prepare time series data and features
  2. Train a machine learning classifier on historical data
  3. Generate trading signals with probability thresholds
  4. Simulate portfolio returns and benchmark against buy-and-hold

docs.title: Key metrics
docs.text: Metric Definition Goal Total return Compound performance of strategy Outperform benchmark Sharpe ratio Risk-adjusted return Maximize Max drawdown Largest peak-to-trough loss Minimize Win rate Percent profitable trades Validate signal quality

docs.title: Example output
docs.text:
Strategy Return: 24.7%
Buy & Hold Return: 18.3%
Sharpe Ratio: 1.23
Max Drawdown: -12.4%
Win Rate: 52.1%

docs.title: Backtesting architecture
docs.text:
• ml_trading_strategy.py orchestrates the full pipeline
  • Feature engineering creates RSI, MACD, volatility, moving averages
  • Cross-validation uses time-aware folds for realistic evaluation
  • Signal persistence and threshold tuning are built into the system

docs.title: Practical considerations
docs.text:
• Avoids look-ahead bias by preserving chronological order
  • Handles NaN values and missing data with careful alignment
  • Compares strategy returns to buy-and-hold for benchmarking
  • Produces decision-ready insights for portfolio managers

Placeholder: Add backtest equity curve visualization or strategy vs benchmark comparison here.

docs.title: Deployment
docs.text: This site is configured for GitHub Pages deployment using MkDocs Material.

docs.title: Local preview
docs.text:
Install dependencies and run the local server:

pip install mkdocs mkdocs-material
mkdocs serve

Then open http://127.0.0.1:8000 in your browser.

docs.title: GitHub Pages deployment
docs.text:
Use MkDocs to publish the site directly to GitHub Pages:

mkdocs gh-deploy --force

This command will build the site and push the generated contents to the gh-pages branch.

docs.title: GitHub Actions
docs.text:
A GitHub Actions workflow is included at:

  • .github/workflows/gh-pages.yml

The workflow automatically:

  • installs Python and site dependencies
  • builds the MkDocs site
  • deploys to GitHub Pages on main branch pushes

docs.title: Site URL
docs.text:
The site is published at:

https://jdurairaj-hub.github.io/ml-portfolio-dalis/

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• mkdocs build validates the configuration locally
  • mkdocs serve enables live editing and preview
  • The deployment workflow is optimized for GitHub Pages

docs.title: Factor Modeling
docs.text: Factor modeling in this portfolio focuses on alternative drivers, correlation structure, and signal construction.

docs.title: Analysis scope
docs.text:
• Macro and sentiment factors from Google Trends and tariff discussions
  • Market factors from technical indicator combinations
  • Statistical correlation using Pearson, Spearman, and Kendall methods

docs.title: Project references
docs.text:
• asset-correlation-analysis/: correlation and factor testing
  • sentiment-analysis/: factor combination with sentiment and technical signals

docs.title: Factor examples
docs.text: Factor Description Use case AI search interest Google Trends volume for AI keywords Tech sector leading indicator Tariff discussion Search volume for tariff-related topics Retail and supply chain risk Momentum Recent returns and moving averages Trend-following signal Volatility Rolling standard deviation Risk-adjusted position sizing

docs.title: Modeling methodology
docs.text:
1. Collect and align feature time series
  2. Compute factor exposures and weights
  3. Test correlation with target returns
  4. Validate statistical significance using p-values

docs.title: Example workflow
docs.text:
aligned = align_series(stock_prices, search_trends)
correlations = compute_correlations(aligned, methods=['pearson', 'spearman', 'kendall'])
results = filter_significant(correlations, alpha=0.05)

docs.title: Research outcomes
docs.text:
• Strong relationships between AI search interest and large-cap tech names
  • Evidence that tariff news correlates with retail performance
  • Modular analysis pipeline ready for lagged factor testing

docs.title: Business relevance
docs.text:
• Investment research: identify persistent factor signals
  • Strategy design: combine macro sentiment with technical momentum
  • Risk monitoring: detect regime shifts in factor correlations

Note: This page is designed to be the quantitative bridge between raw data signals and deployable models.

docs.title: Future Improvements
docs.text: This portfolio is designed to evolve into a production-grade research platform. Future enhancements include both research and deployment upgrades.

docs.title: Research roadmap
docs.text:
• Rolling factor analysis to measure evolving signal strength
  • Lagged predictors to test lead/lag relationships
  • Alternative data integration such as option flow or macro indicators
  • Automated feature selection for model robustness
  • Hyperparameter search for optimal model tuning

docs.title: Productization goals
docs.text:
• API service for live signal requests
  • Deployment pipeline for continuous model updates
  • Dashboard visualization with interactive charts
  • Expanded coverage across asset classes and sectors

docs.title: Technical improvements
docs.text:
• Add mkdocs-material theming and improve site visuals
  • Standardize documentation with a site-wide navigation structure
  • Build GitHub Actions automation for deployment
  • Add test-driven validation for model pipelines

docs.title: Deployment guide
docs.text:
The documentation can be deployed using:

pip install mkdocs mkdocs-material
mkdocs gh-deploy --force

The GitHub Actions workflow at .github/workflows/gh-pages.yml builds and deploys when code is pushed to main.

docs.title: Recruiter-friendly polish
docs.text:
• Maintain concise, actionable bullet points
  • Use tables and example output for readability
  • Emphasize business value and technical contribution
  • Keep the site modern, minimal, and research-focused

This page is the roadmap for the next evolution of the ML + finance portfolio website.

docs.title: Machine Learning Models
docs.text: This portfolio uses machine learning models to predict market direction, forecast volatility, and support strategy decisions.

docs.title: Model families
docs.text:
• Logistic regression for directional trading signals
  • Random Forest and Gradient Boosting for regression and volatility prediction
  • Voting ensembles to stabilize outputs and reduce overfit
  • GARCH models for conditional volatility forecasting

docs.title: Model applications
docs.text: Model type Project Goal Logistic regression prediction-model/ Predict next-day market direction for SPY Random Forest volatility-forecasting/ Forecast future returns and risk factors Gradient Boosting volatility-forecasting/ Capture nonlinear relationships in market data GARCH(1,1) volatility-forecasting/ Model conditional volatility dynamics

docs.title: Feature engineering
docs.text:
Examples of derived features:

  • ret_1d, ret_5d, ret_10d
  • rsi_14, macd, bollinger_band_width
  • volatility_10, volatility_20
  • momentum, trend_strength

docs.title: Training and validation
docs.text:
• Uses time series-aware train/test splits
  • Avoids data leakage through chronological validation
  • Tracks model performance with out-of-sample metrics
  • Stores reusable model artifacts in models/

docs.title: Code sample
docs.text:
model = LogisticRegression(C=0.5, max_iter=2000)
model.fit(X_train, y_train)
probabilities = model.predict_proba(X_test)[:, 1]

docs.title: Portfolio intelligence
docs.text:
ML models in this repo are designed to support:

  • Risk forecasting via combined ML + GARCH methods
  • Strategy signals via probability-based thresholds
  • Research insights through explainable, repeatable pipelines

This page ties together the machine learning narrative across prediction, volatility, and signal modeling.

docs.title: Performance Metrics
docs.text: Performance metrics are the foundation of research validation, risk assessment, and portfolio decision-making.

docs.title: Core metrics
docs.text: Metric Definition Why it matters Sharpe ratio Risk-adjusted return Measures strategy efficiency Max drawdown Largest drawdown Captures worst-case portfolio loss Win rate Percent profitable trades Indicates signal quality VaR Value-at-risk Estimates tail risk Expected return Forecasted return Sets strategy expectations

docs.title: Backtest evaluation
docs.text:
The prediction-model/ project includes:

  • strategy vs benchmark comparison
  • probability-driven signal generation
  • cumulative return curves
  • risk-adjusted performance tables

docs.title: Forecast and simulation metrics
docs.text:
The volatility-forecasting/ project includes:

  • distribution comparisons between simulation methods
  • volatility forecast accuracy
  • probability of profit estimates
  • scenario-based risk diagnostics

docs.title: Statistical validation
docs.text:
This portfolio also emphasizes statistical rigor:

  • significance testing for correlation results
  • multiple correlation methods (Pearson, Spearman, Kendall)
  • cross-validation to avoid overfitting
  • robust metric reporting for reproducibility

docs.title: Example metric summary
docs.text:
Strategy Return: 24.7%
Sharpe Ratio: 1.23
Max Drawdown: -12.4%
Win Rate: 52.1%

docs.title: Practical takeaway
docs.text: High-quality performance reporting helps recruiters and stakeholders understand model quality, downside risk, and whether research has production readiness.

docs.title: Portfolio Overview
docs.text: This portfolio is organized as a professional research site, with each page aligned to core finance and machine learning themes.

docs.title: Portfolio Structure
docs.text:
The repository includes modular projects that can be executed independently while contributing to a cohesive analytics framework.

Area Project Folder Purpose Forecasting volatility-forecasting/ Ensemble ML + GARCH volatility modeling and Monte Carlo simulations Prediction prediction-model/ Long/flat trading strategy for SPY using logistic regression and technical features Sentiment sentiment-analysis/ Hybrid sentiment scanner blending social, news, and technical signals Correlation asset-correlation-analysis/ Statistical analysis of Google Trends, tariffs, and stock price relationships

docs.title: Architecture Principles
docs.text:
• Modular design: Each project folder contains isolated code, data, and pipelines.
  • Reproducibility: Environment variables and requirements are centralized.
  • Data-driven: Metrics, statistical tests, and visual analysis are core outputs.
  • Research-first: Every model is framed with business context and measurable outcomes.

docs.title: Volatility Forecasting
docs.text:
A robust pipeline that combines:

  • Polygon.io data ingestion
  • Technical feature engineering
  • ML regressors and ensemble methods
  • GARCH volatility modeling
  • Monte Carlo scenario simulation

docs.title: Trading Strategy Backtesting
docs.text:
A research-grade backtest that includes:

  • Time series cross-validation
  • Signal probability thresholds
  • Sharpe ratio, max drawdown, and win rate
  • Benchmark comparison vs buy-and-hold

docs.title: Quantitative Sentiment Research
docs.text:
A hybrid framework which blends:

  • Reddit sentiment and comment volume
  • Financial news scoring
  • Technical indicator validation
  • Configurable decision weights

docs.title: Correlation and Factor Modeling
docs.text:
A statistics-first study of:

  • Search interest and equity returns
  • Tariff discussions and retail performance
  • Rolling correlations and significance testing
  • Factor construction for alternative data

docs.title: Portfolio Narrative
docs.text:
This repository is designed to tell a story:

  1. Build trust with clear data sources and pipeline descriptions.
  2. Demonstrate impact through metrics and risk-based evaluation.
  3. Highlight innovation using alternative data and hybrid model design.
  4. Keep it recruiter-friendly with concise sectioning and business relevance.

docs.title: Read the core pages
docs.text:
• Time Series & Forecasting
  • Factor Modeling
  • Backtesting
  • Quantitative Research
  • Machine Learning Models

docs.title: Quantitative Research
docs.text: This portfolio emphasizes quantitative research frameworks that blend market data, sentiment, and statistical validation.

docs.title: Research pillars
docs.text:
• Signal discovery: find edges using alternative data and technical inputs
  • Validation: use statistical tests and backtesting to confirm robustness
  • Interpretation: explain model outcomes with finance context

docs.title: Sentiment and signal fusion
docs.text:
The sentiment-analysis/ toolkit illustrates hybrid decision making:

  • Technical indicators from price action
  • News sentiment from RSS and web sources
  • Social sentiment from Reddit communities
  • Fundamentals and momentum overlays

docs.title: Research workflow
docs.text:
1. Fetch and preprocess multi-source data
  2. Compute sentiment scores and technical metrics
  3. Fuse signals into a unified hybrid score
  4. Output ranked actionable signals with confidence levels

docs.title: Use cases
docs.text:
• Pre-trade research: generate watchlists and hypothesis tests
  • Strategy signals: combine technical and sentiment signals for timing
  • Risk discovery: detect sentiment-driven regime changes

docs.title: Documentation highlights
docs.text:
• Architecture diagrams and pipeline descriptions
  • Command-line examples for reproducible runs
  • Output formats in JSON, CSV, and console tables

docs.title: Example decision matrix
docs.text:
Feature group Weight Commentary Technical score 45% Trend and momentum signal strength Sentiment score 25% News and social market psychology Fundamental score 30% Quality and valuation signal

The research page is designed to show how technical, sentiment, and fundamental signals can be combined into a disciplined quantitative pipeline.

docs.title: Time Series & Forecasting
docs.text: Time series modeling is central to this portfolio. It combines classic financial forecasting with modern machine learning and simulation techniques.

docs.title: Core Projects
docs.text:
• volatility-forecasting/: ML-enhanced volatility prediction with GARCH and Monte Carlo simulation
  • asset-correlation-analysis/: Trend data correlation analysis using Google Trends and stock prices

docs.title: Data pipeline
docs.text:
• Acquire historical OHLCV data from Polygon.io and Yahoo Finance
  • Normalize and align daily and weekly time series
  • Construct technical features and risk indicators

docs.title: Forecast methods
docs.text:
• Traditional Monte Carlo using historical drift and volatility
  • ML-enhanced Monte Carlo leveraging model predicted drift
  • ML + GARCH Monte Carlo combining ML drift with GARCH volatility forecasts

docs.title: Feature engineering
docs.text:
features = [
    'log_return',
    'ma_10',
    'ma_20',
    'volatility_10',
    'volatility_20',
    'momentum',
]

docs.title: Forecast evaluation
docs.text:
Key evaluation steps:

  • Train/test split consistent with time order
  • Compare forecasted return distributions
  • Quantify forecast bias and variance
  • Validate against actual realized volatility

docs.title: Cross-project synergy
docs.text: Forecast focus Related project Volatility and risk distribution volatility-forecasting/ Signal persistence and market psychology asset-correlation-analysis/ Risk-adjusted decision support prediction-model/

docs.title: Outputs and deliverables
docs.text:
• Simulation comparison plots saved to output/
  • Forecast statistics including VaR, expected return, probability of profit
  • ML model performance metrics for regression and volatility prediction

docs.title: Architecture section
docs.text:
1. Data ingestion (src/data.py)
  2. Feature generation (src/features.py)
  3. Model training (src/model.py)
  4. Volatility modeling (src/garch.py)
  5. Simulations (src/simulation.py)
